vulnerability.reference: Researchers treat factorized representations as causally meaningful
vulnerability.generated: Researchers lack ground-truth factor labels, so they cannot detect when the model conflates independent physical processes into one component.

impact.reference: False scientific discoveries waste resources and misdirect future research
impact.generated: False scientific discoveries propagate into literature, corrupting physical theories and wasting follow-up experimental resources.

dose_response.reference: Diffusion models' factorized but non-continuous manifold representations conflating independent scene factors lead to spurious correlations, which when researchers treat these decompositions as causally meaningful, result in false scientific discoveries that waste resources.
dose_response.generated: Diffusion models' factorized but non-continuous manifold representations, through researchers analyzing microscopy or satellite imagery with unsupervised decomposition, trigger undetected conflation of independent physical processes without ground-truth labels, leading to false discoveries that corrupt theories and waste experimental resources.
